meta.name: Tex-Mex Quesadillas
meta.subtitle: Crispy, cheesy tortillas with optional chicken or steak, peppers/onions, and a quick skillet method.

source.name: SSA Canonical Tex-Mex
notes: Designed as a flexible template: cheese-only base + optional protein + optional veg.
Includes griddle/sheet-pan options and kid-friendly mild variant.

yield.notes: Makes 4 quesadillas (8 wedges each if quartered).

summary: A reliable Tex-Mex quesadilla template: melty cheese + crisp tortilla, with optional fajita veg and protein. Great for fast dinners and leftover remixing.

notes: cheesy
toasty
savory
salsa_optional

optional.label: Sheet pan (oven method)
optional.notes: Useful for cooking many quesadillas at once.

ingredients.label: Flour tortillas (8–10 inch)
ingredients.notes: Use burrito-size for fold-over style; use smaller for snack quesadillas.

ingredients.label: Cheddar, shredded (optional blend)
ingredients.notes: Use half Jack + half cheddar for stronger flavor.

ingredients.label: Oaxaca cheese (optional)
ingredients.notes: Great melt; substitute for Jack if preferred.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil
ingredients.notes: Use as needed to lightly coat the pan.

ingredients.label: Black beans, drained (optional)
ingredients.notes: Pat dry a bit so the quesadilla stays crisp.

ingredients.label: Refried beans (optional)
ingredients.notes: Spread thinly; too much makes it hard to crisp.

steps.title: Prep optional fillings (veg/protein)
instructions: If using fajita vegetables: slice pepper and onion (and jalapeño if desired).
If using cooked protein: shred/dice chicken or thinly slice cooked steak.
Keep fillings relatively dry so the quesadilla crisps well.

steps.title: Optional: sauté fajita vegetables
instructions: Heat a skillet over medium-high with a small drizzle of oil.
Add peppers and onions; sauté 4–6 minutes until softened with light browning.
Season with a pinch of taco seasoning (or cumin/chili powder/garlic powder).
Transfer to a plate and wipe the pan if needed.
checks: Veg softened; some browning; not watery

steps.title: Build the quesadilla (fold-over or full-round)
instructions: Fold-over method (fast): sprinkle cheese on one half of each tortilla, add a thin layer of fillings, then top with a bit more cheese and fold shut.
Full-round method (extra even): place one tortilla down, sprinkle cheese, add fillings, add a little more cheese, then top with a second tortilla.
Keep fillings in a thin layer so the cheese can melt before the tortilla over-browns.
checks: Cheese on both sides of fillings; not overstuffed

steps.title: Toast first side until golden and cheese starts melting
instructions: Heat skillet (or griddle) over medium. Add a light film of oil (or a small pat of butter).
Place quesadilla in the pan and cook 2–4 minutes until the underside is golden.
Press gently with a spatula to encourage browning and help the cheese bond.
checks: Underside golden; cheese beginning to melt

steps.title: Flip and finish melting
instructions: Flip carefully (use a wide spatula; for full-round, consider holding the top with your hand or using a plate-assisted flip).
Cook 2–3 minutes more until the second side is golden and the cheese is fully melted.
Transfer to a cutting board and rest 1 minute before slicing.
checks: Both sides crisp; cheese fully melted; structure holds

steps.title: Slice and serve
instructions: Slice into wedges (usually 4 per quesadilla; 6 if very large).
Serve with salsa, sour cream, and/or guacamole. Add cilantro and lime if desired.

steps.title: Oven batch method (optional, feeds a crowd)
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Place a sheet pan inside to preheat (helps crisp).
Assemble quesadillas. Brush lightly with oil or melted butter on the outside.
Place on the hot sheet pan and bake 5 minutes; flip and bake 4–6 minutes more until crisp and melted.

notes: Store wedges airtight.
Re-crisp in a dry skillet over medium heat or air-fryer; avoid microwaving if you want crispness.
Freeze in a single layer, then bag. Reheat from frozen in oven/air-fryer until hot and crisp.
Best with cheese-only or chicken; high-moisture fillings can soften.
If using cooked chicken/steak, ensure it was cooled and stored safely before reusing.
Reheat leftovers until steaming hot.

makeAhead: Prep fajita veg and protein ahead; assemble and cook in 5 minutes.
Shred a block of cheese once for the week to reduce active time.
leftoverRemix: Use leftover fajita meat/veg from fajitas.json.
Add a thin layer of refried beans as a glue and extra protein.

notes: For kids: use small tortillas and mild cheese; skip jalapeño; serve salsa on the side.
For higher protein: add beans + chicken, but keep layers thin.
Skip fillings; use extra cheese for strong melt and structure.
Serve with salsa and/or sour cream.
Sauté peppers/onions first, then add sliced steak or chicken.
Optional: a pinch of taco seasoning in the veg.
Spread refried beans thinly, then add cheese and toast.
Add jalapeño pickles or green chiles if you want heat.
Use certified GF tortillas (often more delicate).
Cook slightly lower and flip carefully to prevent cracking.
